title: Safety fastener
abstract: The present invention concerns a safety fastener and/or a safety system. More particularly, but not exclusively, this invention concerns a safety fastener which indicates when a fastener is damaged, and/or a safety system which indicates when a safety critical component is damaged. The fastener comprises a load bearing body (16), wherein the load bearing body encloses an inner volume filled (12) with fluid, and a fluid sensor (14) configured to detect the presence of the fluid within the inner volume, this fluid sensor being further configured such that in the event of detecting the fluid, a "fluid present" state is activated. If the fastener is damaged, fluid may escape from the inner volume, such that the fluid sensor leaves the "fluid present" state, indicating damage has occurred.
claims: Claims Claims 1. A fastener comprising a load bearing body, wherein the load bearing body encloses an inner volume filled with fluid, and a fluid sensor configured to detect the presence of the fluid within the inner volume, the fluid sensor being further configured such that in the event of detecting the fluid, a âfluid presentâ state is activated. 2. A fastener according to claim 1, wherein the fluid sensor is wired to an external control unit. 3. A fastener according to claim 1 or claim 2, wherein the fluid sensor is wired to an external power supply. 4. A fastener according to claim 1, wherein the fluid sensor is wirelessly connected to an external control unit. 5. A fastener according to claim 1 or claim 4, wherein the fluid sensor is wirelessly connected to an external power supply. 6. A fastener according to claim 1, wherein the fastener comprises an internal power supply connected to the fluid sensor. 7. A fastener according to any preceding claim, wherein the fastener is a pin. 8. A fastener according to any of claims 1 to 6, wherein the fastener is a bolt. 9. A fastener according to any preceding claim, wherein the fluid is held under higher than atmospheric pressure in the inner volume of the load bearing body. 10. A fastener according to any preceding claim, wherein the fluid is a liquid. 11. A fastener according to any preceding claim, wherein the fluid is a gas. 12. A fastener according to any preceding claim, comprising a first part and a second part. 13. A fastener according to claim 12, wherein the first part is mechanically joined to the second part. 14. A fastener according to claim 12 or claim 13, wherein the first part and second part comprise the load bearing body. 15. A fastener according to claim 14, wherein one or both of the first part and second part define the inner volume of the load bearing element. 16. A fastener according to claim 15, wherein one of the first part and second part comprise the fluid sensor. 17. A fastener as claimed in any preceding claim, comprising a plurality of inner volumes and at least one corresponding fluid sensors configured to detect the presence of the fluid within the inner volumes, the at least one fluid sensor being further configured such that in the event of detecting the fluid, a âfluid presentâ state is activated. 18. A fastener as claimed in any preceding claim, wherein an inner volume is located co-axial with the central longitudinal axis of the fastener. 19. A fastener as claimed in claim 17, wherein the plurality of inner volumes are located towards the outer circumference of the fastener. 20. A fastener as claimed in claim 17, wherein the plurality of inner volumes are in fluid communication. 21. A fastener as claimed in claim 17, wherein the plurality of inner volumes are distinct inner volumes that do not connect with each other. 22. A fastener as claimed in any preceding claim, comprising an inner volume and a detector bar located within the inner volume, the detector bar configured such that if the fastener is deformed, the detector bar contacts a surface defining the inner volume thereby prompting a deformation alert or notification to be triggered 23. A method of detecting a crack in a load bearing body of a fastener according to claim 1, the method comprising the steps of observing the fastener, and in the event of observing the fastener is not in the fluid present state, activating an alarm signal. 24. A method as claimed in claim 23, wherein the alarm signal causes a visible or audible alarm to be triggered. 25. A method as claimed in claim 23, wherein the alarm signal causes a notification to be sent to a control unit or a smart device. 25. A method as claimed in claim 23, wherein the alarm signal causes a shutdown of a machine including the fastener. 26. A fastener comprising a load bearing body, the load bearing body comprising an inner volume defined by an internal surface of the load bearing body, and a detector bar is located within in the inner volume, such that, in a normal state, the detector bar does not make contact with the internal surface of the load bearing body, and in a deformed state, the detector bar does make contact with the internal surface of the load bearing body. 27. A fastener as claimed in claim 26, wherein the detector bar comprises a detector contact extending away from the longitudinal axis of the detector bar. 28. A fastener as claimed in claim 26 or 27, wherein the detector bar comprises a plurality of detector contacts located spaced apart along the detector bar. 29. A fastener as claimed in claim 28, wherein the detector bar comprises a first centrally located detector contact and a second detector contact located spaced from the centrally located detector contact 30. A fastener, the fastener comprising a load bearing body, wherein the load bearing body encloses a first inner volume filled with fluid, and a fluid sensor configured to detect the presence of the fluid within the first inner volume, the fluid sensor being further configured such that in the event of detecting the fluid, a âfluid presentâ state is activated, the fastener further comprising a second inner volume, the inner volume being defined by an internal surface of the load bearing body, and a detector bar is located within in the second inner volume, such that, in a normal state, the detector bar does not make contact with the internal surface of the load bearing body, and in a deformed state, the detector bar does make contact with the internal surface of the load bearing body..
